subUnits.title: 12.1 Comprehensive Revision of Trigonometry
notes: - SOH CAH TOA: sin = O/H, cos = A/H, tan = O/A.
- Know how to derive them using isosceles and equilateral triangles.
- Finding missing sides and angles in right-angled triangles.
- Solving real-life word problems (ladders, shadows, heights).
- Know the shape and key points of sine and cosine graphs from 0° to 360°.
- Understand the unit circle and quadrant signs.
- Trigonometry questions are guaranteed in WAEC and NECO. Mastery of SOH CAH TOA and special angles is essential for scoring high.
diagramDescriptions: Summary table of trig ratios and special angles
Summary of sine and cosine graphs
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics comprehensive revision trigonometry SOH CAH TOA graphs site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What does SOH stand for?
quiz.explanation: SOH stands for Sine = Opposite / Hypotenuse.

quiz.q: What is the exact value of sin 60°?
quiz.explanation: The exact value of sin 60° is √3/2.

quiz.q: If tan θ = 1, what is the angle θ?
quiz.explanation: tan 45° is exactly 1.

quiz.q: In a right-angled triangle, the side next to the angle (not the hypotenuse) is the:
quiz.explanation: The side next to the angle is the Adjacent side.

quiz.q: What is the exact value of cos 30°?
quiz.explanation: The exact value of cos 30° is √3/2.

quiz.q: If sin θ = 0.5 and the hypotenuse is 10, what is the opposite side?
At what angle does the sine graph first cross the x-axis after 0°?
quiz.explanation: sin 180° = 0, so it crosses the x-axis at 180°.

quiz.q: What is the maximum value of the cosine graph?
quiz.explanation: The cosine graph peaks at y = 1 at 0° and 360°.

quiz.q: In Quadrant II, which trig ratio is positive?
quiz.explanation: In Q2, only Sine is positive.

quiz.q: To derive the trig ratios for 45°, which triangle is used?
quiz.explanation: An isosceles right-angled triangle is used to derive the 45° ratios.

subUnits.title: 12.2 Comprehensive Revision of Statistics
notes: - Bar charts: Gaps between bars, for discrete/categorical data.
- Histograms: No gaps, continuous data, class boundaries on x-axis.
- Frequency polygons: Line graph of class marks vs frequency.
- Class width: Upper boundary - Lower boundary.
- Statistics questions in WAEC often combine these topics. Ensure you can draw and interpret all these graphs accurately.
diagramDescriptions: Summary table of statistical graphs and their uses
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics comprehensive revision statistics graphs grouped data site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the main visual difference between a bar chart and a histogram?
opts: Histograms have no gaps between bars
quiz.explanation: Histograms have no gaps because they represent continuous data.

quiz.q: What is the formula for a sectoral angle in a pie chart?
quiz.explanation: Sectoral Angle = (Frequency / Total Frequency) × 360°.

quiz.q: What is plotted on the x-axis of a histogram?
quiz.explanation: The continuous class boundaries are plotted on the x-axis.

quiz.q: What is the class mark for the interval 20-29?
What is the modal class?
opts: The class with the lowest frequency
The class with the highest frequency
quiz.explanation: The modal class is the class interval with the highest frequency.

quiz.q: What are the class boundaries for 10-14?
quiz.explanation: Subtract 0.5 from 10 (9.5) and add 0.5 to 14 (14.5).

quiz.q: What is plotted on the x-axis of a frequency polygon?
quiz.explanation: The class marks (midpoints) are plotted on the x-axis.

quiz.q: If a pie chart sector has an angle of 90°, what fraction of the total does it represent?
What is the class width for 50-59?
quiz.explanation: Boundaries are 49.5 - 59.5. Width = 10.

quiz.q: Bar charts are best used for which type of data?
quiz.explanation: Bar charts are ideal for categorical data.

subUnits.title: 12.3 Examination Preparation and Past Question Practice
notes: Examination Preparation and Past Question Practice
- Objective: Quick recall of formulas, special angles, graph properties.
- Theory: Drawing graphs accurately, solving trig problems step-by-step, calculating grouped data statistics.
- Show all working in theory questions.
- Use a sharp pencil and ruler for graphs.
- Label all axes and titles clearly.
- Double-check calculations, especially with trigonometry and class boundaries.
- Practicing past WAEC and NECO questions is the best way to prepare for the format and difficulty level of the actual exam.
diagramDescriptions: Example of a well-drawn graph with labels
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics WAEC NECO exam preparation past questions site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the best way to prepare for the WAEC/NECO math exam?
quiz.explanation: Practicing past questions helps you understand the exam format and question types.

quiz.q: In a theory question, what should you always do?
quiz.explanation: Showing all working ensures you get method marks even if the final answer is wrong.

quiz.q: When drawing a graph, what must you label?
opts: Both axes with titles and scales
quiz.explanation: Both axes must be clearly labeled with titles and scales.

quiz.q: What is the exact value of tan 30°?
quiz.explanation: The exact value of tan 30° is 1/√3 (or √3/3).

quiz.q: If a class interval is 10-19, what is the lower class boundary?
quiz.explanation: Lower boundary = 10 - 0.5 = 9.5.

quiz.q: What is the sum of all sectoral angles in a pie chart?
quiz.explanation: The sum of all sectoral angles must equal exactly 360°.

quiz.q: What is the class mark for 40-49?
In a right-angled triangle, the longest side is the:
quiz.explanation: The hypotenuse is always the longest side.

quiz.q: What is the main visual difference between a bar chart and a histogram?
opts: Histograms have no gaps between bars
quiz.explanation: Histograms have no gaps because they represent continuous data.

quiz.q: What is the exact value of sin 45°?
quiz.explanation: The exact value of sin 45° is √2/2 (or 1/√2).
